# DSP Architecture: ChordGPT

**CRITICAL CONTRACT:** This specification is immutable during Stages 1-4 implementation. Stage 1 Planning cannot proceed without this file. Stage 3 (DSP) implements this exact architecture.

**Generated by:** Stage 0 Research (2026-03-17)
**Referenced by:** Stage 1 (Planning), Stage 3 (DSP Implementation)
**Purpose:** System specification defining audio/MIDI generation components, API integration, synth engine, and signal flow

---

## Core Components

### OpenAI API Client

- **JUCE Class:** `juce::URL` with `withPOSTData()` and `InputStreamOptions.withExtraHeaders()` — custom implementation wrapping JUCE networking
- **Purpose:** Send conversation history + user prompt to OpenAI Chat Completions API; receive structured chord JSON response
- **Parameters Affected:** temperature (sent as API parameter), apiKey (Authorization header)
- **Configuration:**
  - Endpoint: `https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions`
  - Method: POST with JSON body
  - Headers: `Content-Type: application/json\r\nAuthorization: Bearer {apiKey}`
  - Model: gpt-4o (configurable, hardcoded default)
  - Response format: JSON schema enforcement via system prompt (structured outputs)
  - Threading: Background `std::thread` for non-blocking calls; `juce::AsyncUpdater::triggerAsyncUpdate()` for safe UI callbacks back on message thread
  - macOS ATS: HTTPS only (HTTP blocked by App Transport Security on macOS)

---

### Conversation Memory Manager

- **JUCE Class:** Custom implementation using `std::vector<juce::var>` (array of message objects)
- **Purpose:** Maintain full conversation history (all prior user prompts + assistant chord responses) for harmonic continuity across API calls
- **Parameters Affected:** None (not APVTS; stored in plugin state)
- **Configuration:**
  - Each entry: `{ "role": "user", "content": "dark forest scene" }` or `{ "role": "assistant", "content": "{chord_json}" }`
  - System message: Badalamenti-style system prompt instructing harmonic continuity and structured JSON response format
  - "New Scene" button clears this array (but keeps temperature/voicing settings)
  - Token budget: ~20-30 exchanges before hitting practical limits; chord-only responses stay compact (~100 tokens/exchange)
  - Serialization: Saved to plugin state as JSON string (not DAW project file)

---

### Chord Voicing Engine

- **JUCE Class:** Custom implementation (no JUCE class for this)
- **Purpose:** Transform a set of chord intervals into MIDI note numbers distributed across octaves according to the selected voicing style
- **Parameters Affected:** voicing (Close, Open, Spread, Drop2)
- **Configuration:**
  - Interval lookup table (chord type → semitone intervals from root):
    - maj: `[0, 4, 7]`
    - min: `[0, 3, 7]`
    - maj7: `[0, 4, 7, 11]`
    - m7: `[0, 3, 7, 10]`
    - dom7: `[0, 4, 7, 10]`
    - m7b5: `[0, 3, 6, 10]`
    - dim7: `[0, 3, 6, 9]`
    - maj9: `[0, 4, 7, 11, 14]`
    - m9: `[0, 3, 7, 10, 14]`
    - dom9: `[0, 4, 7, 10, 14]`
    - sus2: `[0, 2, 7]`
    - sus4: `[0, 5, 7]`
    - aug: `[0, 4, 8]`
  - Close voicing: Stack all intervals within one octave above root (e.g., Cmaj7 root=C4=60 → [60, 64, 67, 71])
  - Open voicing: Distribute notes across two octaves — alternate high/low placement to spread intervals
  - Spread voicing: Maximum register separation — bass note low, upper voices widely spaced
  - Drop2 voicing: Close position → transpose 2nd-highest note down one octave (classic jazz voicing)
  - Base octave: MIDI note 48 (C3) for root in Close mode; adjustable register center

---

### MIDI Output Generator

- **JUCE Class:** `juce::MidiBuffer`, `juce::MidiMessage`
- **Purpose:** Queue chord note-on/note-off events for output via the plugin's MIDI output, routable to any DAW instrument
- **Parameters Affected:** voicing (note selection affects which notes are output)
- **Configuration:**
  - Note-on events generated at sample position 0 of the next processBlock() call after chord trigger
  - Note-off events generated at a fixed 4-second hold duration (configurable in code constant)
  - Uses a lock-free `std::atomic` flag to signal "new chord ready" from message thread to audio thread
  - Chord notes stored in `std::array<int, 8>` (max 8 notes) with note count atomic
  - MIDI channel: 1 (hardcoded)
  - producesMidi: true — declared in plugin CMakeLists.txt and processor capabilities

---

### Piano Synth Engine

- **JUCE Class:** `juce::Synthesiser` + `juce::SynthesiserSound` + `juce::SynthesiserVoice` (custom SimplePianoVoice adapted from JazzGptMidi)
- **Purpose:** Built-in polyphonic piano preview — plays the generated chord immediately without external instrument
- **Parameters Affected:** None (always active for preview; volume is fixed internal level)
- **Configuration:**
  - Voice count: 16 voices (polyphonic — can sustain previous chord while new chord plays)
  - Synthesis: Additive sine oscillators (fundamental + 2nd harmonic at 0.3 + 3rd harmonic at 0.12) with ADSR envelope
  - ADSR: Attack=8ms, Decay=800ms, Sustain=0.4, Release=500ms (piano-style)
  - Filter: Low-pass (1200Hz low register / 2000Hz high register) + high-shelf (3kHz, -6dB)
  - Adapted directly from JazzGptMidi SimplePianoVoice (proven pattern in this codebase)
  - All notes off when "New Scene" clears history (avoids hanging notes)
  - renderNextBlock() called in processBlock() on audio thread

---

### State Persistence Manager

- **JUCE Class:** `juce::ValueTree`, `juce::JSON`, `juce::MemoryBlock`
- **Purpose:** Persist API key, temperature, voicing, and conversation history across DAW sessions (API key stored separately from DAW project file)
- **Parameters Affected:** apiKey, temperature, voicing, conversationHistory
- **Configuration:**
  - APVTS: NOT used for any of these parameters (as specified in brief)
  - temperature: Stored as float in plugin state ValueTree
  - voicing: Stored as int (0=Close, 1=Open, 2=Spread, 3=Drop2) in plugin state ValueTree
  - apiKey: Stored in plugin state, NOT exported to DAW project file (security requirement)
  - conversationHistory: Serialized as JSON string in plugin state ValueTree
  - getStateInformation() / setStateInformation() serialize/restore all state
  - On restore: validate JSON, restore conversation array, update UI via evaluateJavascript()

---

## Processing Chain

This is NOT a traditional audio DSP chain. ChordGPT is a MIDI Generator + Instrument with the following data flow:

```
User Text Input (UI)
  ↓
WebView → Native Function Bridge
  ↓
C++: PluginProcessor::requestChord(userPrompt)
  ↓
[Background Thread]
  ↓
Conversation Memory Manager
(append user message, build full messages array)
  ↓
OpenAI API Client
(POST to api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions with full history)
  ↓
[API Response — Background Thread]
  ↓
JSON Parser (juce::JSON::parse, extract chord_name + midi_notes)
  ↓
Chord Voicing Engine
(apply voicing algorithm to midi_notes from API)
  ↓
Conversation Memory Manager
(append assistant response to history)
  ↓
AsyncUpdater::triggerAsyncUpdate()
  ↓
[Message Thread Callback]
  ↓
+----------------------------------+
|                                  |
v                                  v
MIDI Output Queue              Piano Synth Trigger
(atomic flag + note array)     (pianoSynth.noteOn() for each note)
  |                                  |
  v                                  v
[Audio Thread — processBlock()]    [Audio Thread — processBlock()]
  |                                  |
  v                                  v
juce::MidiBuffer                juce::Synthesiser::renderNextBlock()
(note-on/off injected)          (piano audio rendered)
  |                                  |
  +----------------------------------+
                    |
                    v
            DAW MIDI Output          Stereo Audio Output
            (routable to any         (built-in piano preview)
             DAW instrument)
```

**Routing notes:**
- No audio INPUT — ChordGPT is an instrument (output-only bus configuration)
- Two parallel outputs: MIDI data (to DAW) + audio (piano synth preview)
- API call is async — UI shows loading state while waiting
- Chord notes flow through both MIDI output AND piano synth simultaneously
- "New Scene" clears conversation memory and calls allNotesOff() on synth

---

## System Architecture

### HTTP API System

**Endpoint:** OpenAI Chat Completions API (`https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions`)

**Request format:**
```json
{
  "model": "gpt-4o",
  "temperature": 0.7,
  "messages": [
    { "role": "system", "content": "...Badalamenti system prompt..." },
    { "role": "user", "content": "dark forest, danger" },
    { "role": "assistant", "content": "{\"chord_name\":\"Dm9\",\"midi_notes\":[50,53,57,60,64],\"emotional_reasoning\":\"Minor ninth for dread\"}" },
    { "role": "user", "content": "the creature emerges" }
  ]
}
```

**Response schema (enforced via system prompt JSON instructions):**
```json
{
  "chord_name": "string — e.g. Dm9",
  "midi_notes": "array of int — 4-7 MIDI note numbers",
  "voicing_type": "string — describes voicing applied",
  "emotional_reasoning": "string — brief explanation for display"
}
```

**JUCE networking implementation:**
- `juce::URL("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions")`
- `.withPOSTData(jsonBody)`
- `createInputStream(juce::URL::InputStreamOptions(juce::URL::ParameterHandling::inPostData).withExtraHeaders("Content-Type: application/json\r\nAuthorization: Bearer " + apiKey))`
- Error handling: Check for null stream, parse error codes from response body
- Timeout: 30 seconds (configurable constant)

**Threading:**
- API call runs on `std::thread` (background) — NEVER on audio thread
- Result posted back to message thread via `juce::AsyncUpdater`
- In-flight request tracked with `std::atomic<bool> apiCallInProgress`
- UI shows spinner/loading state while awaiting response

**macOS ATS:**
- HTTPS required (ATS blocks HTTP on macOS)
- VST3 has fewer sandbox restrictions than AU — test both formats
- No special entitlements needed for HTTPS with standard URL class

---

### MIDI Routing

**Plugin declaration:** `producesMidi() → true`, `acceptsMidi() → false`

**MIDI generation in processBlock():**
```cpp
if (newChordReady.load())
{
    // Clear previous chord notes
    for (int note : activeChordNotes)
        midiMessages.addEvent(juce::MidiMessage::noteOff(1, note), 0);

    // Add new chord note-ons
    int noteCount = currentNoteCount.load();
    for (int i = 0; i < noteCount; ++i)
    {
        midiMessages.addEvent(
            juce::MidiMessage::noteOn(1, currentChordNotes[i], (juce::uint8)80), 0);
    }

    // Schedule note-offs after hold duration
    notesHeldSamples = 0;
    holdDurationSamples = static_cast<int>(getSampleRate() * CHORD_HOLD_SECONDS);
    activeChordNotes = currentChordNotes;
    newChordReady.store(false);
}

// Release held notes after hold duration
if (notesHeldSamples >= holdDurationSamples && !activeChordNotes.empty())
{
    for (int note : activeChordNotes)
        midiMessages.addEvent(juce::MidiMessage::noteOff(1, note), 0);
    activeChordNotes.clear();
}
notesHeldSamples += buffer.getNumSamples();
```

**Note:** The MIDI output goes to the DAW's MIDI routing. The DAW can route this to any instrument track. Simultaneously, the piano synth renders audio preview directly.

---

### State Persistence

**What state is saved:**
- temperature (float, 0.0-1.5)
- voicing (int, 0-3)
- conversationHistory (JSON string — full message array)
- apiKey (string — stored in plugin state, NOT exported to host project)

**Security note on API key:**
- The brief explicitly states the API key must NOT be embedded in the DAW project file
- Implementation: Serialize apiKey only to plugin's own state storage, with a flag that prevents getStateInformation() from including it in DAW-exportable data
- Alternative: Store in a separate local preferences file using `juce::PropertiesFile`

**Serialization format:**
```xml
<ChordGPTState>
  <Temperature value="0.7"/>
  <Voicing value="0"/>
  <ConversationHistory json="[{...}, {...}]"/>
</ChordGPTState>
```

**JUCE classes:**
- `juce::ValueTree` — Custom state tree
- `juce::JSON::parse()` / `juce::JSON::toString()` — Conversation history serialization
- `juce::MemoryBlock` — Binary serialization wrapper for getStateInformation()

**Restore behavior:**
- Invalid JSON in conversationHistory: Clear history, start fresh
- Missing apiKey: Leave empty, user re-enters in UI
- Invalid temperature/voicing: Use defaults (0.7, Close)

---

## Parameter Mapping

| Parameter ID | Type | Range | Component | Usage |
|-------------|------|-------|-----------|-------|
| temperature | Float (plugin state) | 0.0–1.5 | OpenAI API Client | Sent as `temperature` in API request body |
| voicing | Int (plugin state) | 0=Close, 1=Open, 2=Spread, 3=Drop2 | Chord Voicing Engine | Applied to midi_notes after API response |
| apiKey | String (plugin state) | Free text | OpenAI API Client | Authorization Bearer header |
| conversationHistory | JSON array (plugin state) | N/A | Conversation Memory Manager | Full messages[] array sent with each request |

**Note:** None of these are APVTS parameters. They are stored in plugin state and communicated through the WebView native function bridge. This is consistent with the brief and with JazzGptMidi's architecture.

---

## Algorithm Details

### OpenAI Chat Completions API Call

**Algorithm:** HTTP POST to Chat Completions endpoint with full conversation context

**System prompt design:**
```
You are Angelo Badalamenti — a film composer who translates scenes and moods into single piano chords.
The user will describe a scene, emotion, or feeling in freeform text.
You respond ONLY with a JSON object in this exact format:
{
  "chord_name": "Name of chord (e.g. Dm9, Fmaj7#11)",
  "midi_notes": [array of 4-7 MIDI note numbers],
  "voicing_type": "Brief voicing description",
  "emotional_reasoning": "One sentence explaining the harmonic choice"
}
Previous chords in this conversation represent the harmonic story so far.
Choose harmonically coherent continuations. Be musically inventive but serve the narrative.
Return ONLY valid JSON. No preamble. No explanation outside the JSON.
```

**Implementation notes:**
- Build messages array: [system_message, ...conversationHistory, new_user_message]
- Serialize to JSON string using `juce::JSON::toString()`
- POST via `juce::URL::createInputStream()`
- Parse response: extract `choices[0].message.content`, then `juce::JSON::parse()` that string
- Validate: chord_name (non-empty string), midi_notes (array of ints 0-127, count 1-8)
- On parse failure: Show error in UI, do NOT add failed exchange to conversation history
- Error codes: 401 (bad API key), 429 (rate limited), 500 (OpenAI error) — display human-readable messages

---

### Chord Voicing Engine

**Algorithm:** Interval table lookup + register distribution algorithm

**Voicing implementations:**

**Close voicing:**
```
root_midi = root_note + (4 * 12)  // e.g., C4 = MIDI 60
notes = [root_midi + interval for each interval in chord_intervals]
// All notes constrained within one octave of root
```

**Drop2 voicing:**
```
start_with_close_voicing()
sort notes ascending
second_highest = notes[notes.size() - 2]
notes[notes.size() - 2] = second_highest - 12  // drop octave
re-sort ascending
```

**Open voicing:**
```
place root at base octave (e.g., MIDI 48)
place 3rd up 1 octave (+ 12 from close)
place 5th at base + close interval
place 7th up 1 octave
// Alternate high-low to create open spacing
```

**Spread voicing:**
```
place root at bass register (MIDI 36-48)
evenly distribute remaining notes across 2-3 octaves
// Maximum register separation, cinematic texture
```

**Implementation notes:**
- GPT returns midi_notes from API; voicing engine may re-interpret as intervals from detected root
- Alternatively: Trust GPT's midi_notes directly (GPT is instructed to apply voicing in system prompt based on voicing parameter sent in request context)
- Recommended approach: Send voicing type in API request context so GPT applies it natively, then apply JUCE-side voicing as a post-processing correction/normalization pass
- Register center: Middle of piano (MIDI 48-72 range) unless Spread uses lower bass

---

### MIDI Queue (Lock-Free Thread Communication)

**Algorithm:** Atomic flag + fixed-size array for note data

**Implementation notes:**
- `std::atomic<bool> newChordReady` — set true by message thread, cleared by audio thread
- `std::array<int, 8> pendingChordNotes` — written by message thread (while newChordReady is false)
- `std::atomic<int> pendingNoteCount` — written by message thread
- Audio thread polls `newChordReady` at top of each processBlock()
- Lock-free: message thread only writes when `newChordReady == false`; audio thread only reads when `newChordReady == true`
- Simple double-buffer not needed given low update frequency (once per user prompt)

---

### Piano Synth (Adapted from JazzGptMidi)

**Algorithm:** Additive synthesis (sine oscillators) + ADSR + IIR filters

**Implementation notes:**
- Direct adaptation of `SimplePianoVoice` from JazzGptMidi (proven, working code in this codebase)
- `juce::Synthesiser` with 16 voices handles polyphony automatically
- Voice stealing: JUCE Synthesiser handles stealing oldest voice when voice limit reached
- `renderNextBlock()` called in processBlock() on audio thread — no threading issues
- `setCurrentPlaybackSampleRate()` called in prepareToPlay()
- `allNotesOff(0, true)` called on "New Scene" to clear ringing chords
- Note velocity: Fixed at 80 (moderate) for predictable piano preview level
- Audio output: Stereo (same sample written to both channels)

---

## Integration Points

### Feature Dependencies

- **Conversation Memory Manager** → **OpenAI API Client**: History must be assembled before API call is made
- **OpenAI API Client** → **Chord Voicing Engine**: midi_notes from API response are input to voicing engine
- **Chord Voicing Engine** → **MIDI Output Generator**: Voiced notes populate the pending chord array
- **Chord Voicing Engine** → **Piano Synth Engine**: Same voiced notes trigger piano voices
- **State Persistence** → **Conversation Memory Manager**: Conversation history is restored from state on plugin load
- **State Persistence** → **OpenAI API Client**: API key and temperature are read from state for each request
- **"New Scene" Action** → **Conversation Memory Manager**: Clears conversation array
- **"New Scene" Action** → **Piano Synth Engine**: Calls allNotesOff()

---

### Parameter Interactions

- **temperature** affects API response character only — does NOT affect chord voicing calculation
- **voicing** affects Chord Voicing Engine output — changes note distribution but not the harmonic content from GPT
- **apiKey** blocks all API calls if empty — UI should disable submit button when apiKey is empty
- **conversationHistory** grows with each exchange — longer history = richer harmonic continuity but more tokens sent per request
- No APVTS parameters — no host automation, no parameter smoothing needed

---

### Processing Order Requirements

**Per-chord-request sequence (async, not per-sample):**

1. User types prompt text and submits
2. UI validates apiKey is set (non-empty) — block if missing
3. `requestChord(userPrompt)` called from message thread
4. Conversation Memory Manager assembles messages[] array (history + new user message)
5. Background thread starts API call with assembled context
6. UI enters "loading" state (disable submit button, show indicator)
7. API response received on background thread
8. JSON parsed: extract chord_name, midi_notes
9. Chord Voicing Engine applies voicing to midi_notes (may adjust register)
10. Conversation Memory Manager appends [user: prompt] + [assistant: json] to history
11. AsyncUpdater posts result to message thread
12. Message thread: update UI chord log (display chord_name + notes + emotional_reasoning)
13. Message thread: set `newChordReady = true` and populate pending note array
14. Message thread: trigger piano synth noteOn() for each note
15. Audio thread (next processBlock): drain pending chord notes into MidiBuffer
16. Audio thread: render piano synth audio

**Critical order requirements:**
- Step 4 (assemble history) MUST happen before step 5 (API call) — race condition risk if concurrent requests allowed
- Step 10 (append to history) MUST happen AFTER successful JSON parse — don't add invalid responses to history
- Only one API call in-flight at a time — `std::atomic<bool> apiCallInProgress` guards against concurrent requests

---

### Thread Boundaries

**Message thread (UI):**
- Button click handler: calls `requestChord()`
- AsyncUpdater callback: updates UI, triggers piano noteOn, sets newChordReady flag
- State save/restore: `getStateInformation()` / `setStateInformation()`
- "New Scene" action: clear conversation array, call allNotesOff()

**Background thread:**
- `std::thread` spawned for each API call
- Performs: JSON serialization, HTTP POST via juce::URL, response parsing
- Communicates back to message thread via `juce::AsyncUpdater::triggerAsyncUpdate()`
- NEVER touches audio buffers or MIDI buffers

**Audio thread (processBlock):**
- Polls `newChordReady` atomic flag
- Reads `pendingChordNotes` array (written only when newChordReady==false)
- Writes to MidiBuffer (host-provided)
- Calls `pianoSynth.renderNextBlock()` (audio synthesis)
- NEVER allocates memory
- NEVER makes network calls
- NEVER acquires locks (except for juce::Synthesiser's internal lock — acceptable)

**Thread safety guarantees:**
- `std::atomic<bool> newChordReady` — lock-free message thread→audio thread signal
- `std::atomic<bool> apiCallInProgress` — prevents concurrent API calls from message thread
- Piano synth note triggers: Called from message thread (safe — juce::Synthesiser handles thread-safe note triggering via internal lock)
- Conversation history: Only accessed on message thread (not audio thread) — no lock needed

---

## Implementation Risks

### OpenAI HTTP Networking in VST Plugin

**Complexity:** MEDIUM

**Risk Level:** MEDIUM

**Risk factors:**
1. macOS ATS (App Transport Security) blocks non-HTTPS connections — must use HTTPS
2. VST3 sandbox restrictions may affect network access (less severe than AU sandbox)
3. `juce::URL::createInputStream()` is blocking — MUST be called on background thread, not message thread or audio thread
4. API key storage security — must not leak into DAW project file
5. Response parsing robustness — GPT can produce malformed JSON occasionally

**Alternative approaches:**
1. **System prompt JSON enforcement only:** Instruct GPT strictly to return JSON — no formal OpenAI structured_outputs schema. Simpler, works with all GPT-4 variants.
2. **OpenAI structured_outputs feature:** Use `response_format: { type: "json_schema", json_schema: {...} }` — guarantees valid JSON but only works with specific model versions (gpt-4o-2024-08-06+)

**Fallback architecture:**
- Primary: System prompt JSON instruction (simpler, more compatible)
- Fallback: If GPT produces malformed JSON repeatedly → add retry logic (up to 2 retries per request)
- Final fallback: If API unreachable → display error in UI, allow user to retry manually

**Mitigation strategy:**
- Use proven JazzGptMidi HTTP pattern (already in codebase) as reference
- Wrap createInputStream() call in try/catch and null checks
- Validate JSON response before adding to history
- Display error codes human-readably (401=check API key, 429=rate limited, 500=OpenAI error)

---

### Conversation History Token Management

**Complexity:** LOW

**Risk Level:** LOW

**Risk factors:**
1. Very long sessions (50+ exchanges) may approach token limits for context window
2. Large conversation history increases API call latency

**Alternative approaches:**
1. **Full history always:** Send all prior exchanges — ensures maximum harmonic continuity (recommended)
2. **Sliding window:** Keep only last N exchanges (e.g., last 10) — limits token usage but loses early context

**Fallback architecture:**
- Primary: Full history always
- Fallback: If user reports slow responses or token errors → implement configurable sliding window (last 15 exchanges default)

---

### Chord Voicing Algorithm

**Complexity:** LOW-MEDIUM

**Risk Level:** LOW

**Risk factors:**
1. GPT may return notes outside expected octave ranges — need bounds clamping (0-127)
2. Voicing algorithm edge cases: 3-note chords vs 7-note extended chords behave differently
3. Drop2 on 3-note chord is undefined — need fallback (use Close voicing for triads)

**Alternative approaches:**
1. **Trust GPT's notes fully:** System prompt specifies voicing style, GPT applies it — no JUCE-side voicing needed
2. **JUCE-side voicing only:** Build chord from root + interval table, apply voicing algorithmically — independent of GPT's note choices
3. **Hybrid (recommended):** GPT provides midi_notes with voicing context in prompt; JUCE engine applies register normalization and clamps to valid range

**Fallback architecture:**
- If voicing algorithm fails → default to Close voicing always

---

### MIDI Output Generation

**Complexity:** LOW

**Risk Level:** LOW

**Risk factors:**
1. `producesMidi() = true` must be declared — DAW-side MIDI routing depends on this
2. Note-off timing must be managed (holding chord for configurable duration)
3. Previous chord notes must be turned off before new chord plays (no hanging notes)

**Alternative approaches:**
- Standard JUCE MidiBuffer injection in processBlock() — well-documented, proven pattern

**Fallback architecture:**
- Not needed — this is a straightforward pattern with no complex alternatives

---

### Piano Synth Engine

**Complexity:** LOW

**Risk Level:** LOW

**Risk factors:**
1. Voice stealing may cause audible artifacts when new chord is played before previous chord releases

**Mitigation:**
- 16 voices is sufficient for 7-note chords plus previous chord tail
- JUCE Synthesiser handles voice stealing gracefully

---

### API Key Security

**Complexity:** LOW

**Risk Level:** MEDIUM (user trust issue, not technical)

**Risk factors:**
1. If API key leaks into DAW project file (.als, .logicx), it could be unintentionally shared
2. Plugin state serialized via `getStateInformation()` is included in DAW project saves

**Mitigation strategy:**
- Option A: Use `juce::PropertiesFile` (OS keychain–adjacent local preferences file) for API key storage — completely separate from plugin state/DAW project
- Option B: Serialize API key in getStateInformation() but document clearly that users should not share project files
- **Recommended:** Option A — use `juce::ApplicationProperties` / `juce::PropertiesFile` for API key, separate from APVTS/ValueTree state
- This matches the brief's explicit security requirement

---

### Overall Project Risk

**Overall complexity:** MEDIUM
- HTTP networking (MEDIUM) + conversation state (LOW) + chord voicing (LOW) + MIDI output (LOW) + piano synth (LOW — reuse from JazzGptMidi)
- Primary challenge is async API integration, not DSP complexity

**Highest risk component:** OpenAI HTTP Networking
- Represents ~60% of project risk
- Most complex due to threading, error handling, and macOS sandboxing
- All other components are low-complexity or directly reused from JazzGptMidi

**Recommended approach:**
1. Start with piano synth (copy from JazzGptMidi — immediate win)
2. Implement chord voicing engine in isolation (testable without API)
3. Implement MIDI output queue (simple atomic pattern)
4. Build API client with hardcoded test chord (validate networking layer)
5. Add conversation memory and full prompt assembly
6. Wire up WebView UI and all native function bridges

---

## Architecture Decisions

### No APVTS Parameters

**Decision:** Store temperature, voicing, and apiKey in plugin state (ValueTree) rather than APVTS

**Rationale:**
- Temperature and voicing are not automatable DSP parameters — they configure AI behavior
- API key is a string, not a float — APVTS only supports float parameters
- Conversation history is a complex JSON structure — incompatible with APVTS
- JazzGptMidi uses the same pattern successfully (custom state, not APVTS)

**Alternatives considered:**
1. **Float parameter for temperature via APVTS:** Would enable host automation of creativity — not a useful feature, adds complexity
2. **Int parameter for voicing via APVTS:** Would enable voicing automation — not a useful feature for this compositional tool

**Tradeoffs accepted:**
- No host automation of temperature or voicing — acceptable for a compositional sketch tool
- Manual WebView bridge for state sync — more code, but cleaner separation

---

### Instrument Bus Configuration (Output Only)

**Decision:** `BusesProperties().withOutput("Output", juce::AudioChannelSet::stereo(), true)` — no input bus

**Rationale:**
- ChordGPT generates audio from scratch (piano synth) — no audio input needed
- ChordGPT is a MIDI generator + instrument, not an effect
- Correct bus config prevents "missing input" errors in DAWs (see juce8-critical-patterns.md)

**Critical patterns applied:**
- `IS_SYNTH TRUE` in CMakeLists.txt (see pattern #22)
- `NEEDS_MIDI_INPUT FALSE` (ChordGPT does not accept incoming MIDI — it generates MIDI)
- `NEEDS_WEB_BROWSER TRUE` for WebView UI (see pattern #9)
- Output-only BusesProperties matches IS_SYNTH TRUE declaration

---

### Reuse JazzGptMidi Piano Synth

**Decision:** Adapt SimplePianoVoice directly from JazzGptMidi source rather than building new synth

**Rationale:**
- JazzGptMidi's SimplePianoVoice is proven, tested, and working in this codebase
- Identical use case: polyphonic piano preview of AI-generated chord/note content
- No JUCE class for synthesis exists that would be simpler (juce::dsp::Oscillator is mono, not polyphonic)
- Saves significant implementation time for a non-differentiating component

**Tradeoffs accepted:**
- Piano timbre is synthetic (additive sine), not sampled — adequate for compositional sketching
- No per-note velocity variation in chord preview — all notes at velocity 80

---

### Hybrid Voicing: GPT + JUCE Post-Processing

**Decision:** Instruct GPT to apply voicing in system prompt context, then apply JUCE-side register normalization

**Rationale:**
- GPT understands musical voicing concepts and can apply them contextually
- JUCE-side normalization ensures notes are within valid MIDI range (0-127) regardless of GPT output
- Reduces complexity of JUCE voicing engine (no need to parse chord types from chord_name string)

**Alternatives considered:**
1. **Pure JUCE voicing:** GPT returns only chord type, JUCE builds notes from interval table — more deterministic, but loses GPT's contextual voicing intelligence
2. **Pure GPT voicing:** Trust GPT's midi_notes entirely — risk of out-of-range notes or inconsistent register

---

### WebView UI Architecture

**Decision:** WebView-based UI using JUCE's native integration (same as JazzGptMidi)

**Rationale:**
- Dark cinematic UI with text input field, chord history log, and interactive controls is best served by HTML/CSS/JS
- JazzGptMidi demonstrates this pattern works well for text-input-centric plugin UIs
- WebView handles complex layout (scrollable log, multi-line text input) better than native JUCE components
- `.withNativeFunction()` bridge handles all UI→C++ communication (chord request, new scene, settings)

**JUCE critical patterns applied:**
- `NEEDS_WEB_BROWSER TRUE` (pattern #9)
- `std::unique_ptr` member initialization order (pattern #11)
- Explicit URL mapping in getResource() (pattern #8)
- `check_native_interop.js` included (pattern #13)
- `type="module"` on script tags (pattern #21)

**Note:** ChordGPT has NO APVTS parameters, so no WebSliderRelay / WebSliderParameterAttachment needed — UI state communicated entirely via native functions and evaluateJavascript()

---

## Special Considerations

### Thread Safety

- API calls on background thread ONLY — never block message thread or audio thread
- `std::atomic<bool> apiCallInProgress` prevents concurrent requests
- `std::atomic<bool> newChordReady` + fixed-size note array = lock-free audio thread communication
- Piano synth noteOn() called from message thread (AsyncUpdater callback) — safe per JUCE Synthesiser design
- Conversation history accessed only on message thread — no concurrent modification risk

### Performance

- Piano synth: ~5-10% CPU (16-voice additive synth, short attack/decay)
- MIDI output generation: <1% CPU (simple array copy)
- No DSP processing — this plugin renders audio only from synth voices
- API calls: background thread, no CPU impact on audio thread
- Total audio thread CPU: very low — primarily pianoSynth.renderNextBlock()

### Denormal Protection

- `juce::ScopedNoDenormals noDenormals` in processBlock() (standard pattern)
- Piano synth uses phase-based oscillators — denormals unlikely but protection is free

### Sample Rate Handling

- `pianoSynth.setCurrentPlaybackSampleRate(sampleRate)` in prepareToPlay()
- No other sample-rate-dependent calculations
- MIDI output is sample-position-based (position 0 for note-ons) — no rate dependency

### Latency

- Zero latency processing — no lookahead, no buffering in the signal path
- API call latency: 500ms-3s (network dependent) — managed asynchronously, no audio impact
- `getLatencySamples() = 0` — no host latency compensation needed

### macOS Sandbox

- HTTPS required for network calls (ATS enforcement)
- VST3 has fewer sandbox restrictions than AU for network access
- No file system access needed (no local sample files)
- API key stored in plugin state or PropertiesFile (no special filesystem permissions needed)

---

## Research References

### Professional Plugins Researched

1. **MIDI Agent (midiagent.com)**
   - OpenAI/Claude/Gemini powered MIDI generator VST plugin
   - Natural language prompts → MIDI data in DAW
   - Supports multiple LLM providers
   - Observed: Text-to-MIDI pattern validated as viable VST plugin architecture

2. **ChordSeqAI (chordseqai.com)**
   - AI-powered chord progression copilot
   - Focus on chord suggestions with theory awareness
   - Observed: Chord-centric AI tools have real market demand

3. **Orb Producer Suite (Orb Chords)**
   - AI MIDI generator for chord progressions
   - Parameters: Complexity, Density, Polyphony — maps to voicing concept
   - Observed: Chord generation with voicing control is an established plugin pattern

4. **AudioCipher v4.0**
   - Text-to-MIDI with chord inversions and voice leading
   - Demonstrates voicing algorithms (inversions, smooth voice leading) in commercial plugins

5. **JazzGptMidi (existing in this codebase)**
   - Text → OpenAI API → full composition → MIDI output + piano synth
   - Direct architectural predecessor — WebView UI, native functions, piano synth, HTTP networking all proven
   - ChordGPT is a focused, single-chord-at-a-time refinement of this concept

### JUCE Documentation Notes

- **`juce::URL`**: Core HTTP client. `withPOSTData()` + `InputStreamOptions.withExtraHeaders()` for POST with JSON body and Authorization header. Thread: call `createInputStream()` on background thread only.
- **`juce::Synthesiser`**: Polyphonic synth engine. `renderNextBlock()` on audio thread. `noteOn()` / `noteOff()` can be called from message thread (internally thread-safe).
- **`juce::MidiBuffer`**: Buffer of MIDI events. Populated in processBlock() for host MIDI output.
- **`juce::MidiMessage`**: Individual MIDI event creation. `noteOn(channel, note, velocity)` + `noteOff(channel, note)`.
- **`juce::AsyncUpdater`**: Post callbacks to message thread from background thread. `triggerAsyncUpdate()` (background) → `handleAsyncUpdate()` (message thread).
- **`juce::JSON`**: JSON parsing/creation. `juce::JSON::parse(string)` returns `juce::var`. `juce::var::toString()` / `juce::var::operator[]` for access.
- **`juce::ValueTree`**: Hierarchical state storage for plugin state serialization.
- **`juce::WebBrowserComponent`**: WebView component. `.withNativeFunction()` for C++/JS bridge. `.evaluateJavascript()` for C++→JS callbacks.

### Technical Resources

- JUCE Forum: "Using the JUCE URL Reference to connect to the open AI GPT API" — thread ID 59007 — validated juce::URL approach for OpenAI
- JUCE Forum: "SOLVED: Using juce::URL to send POST with body" — thread ID 59701 — confirms withPOSTData() pattern
- OpenAI API docs: Chat Completions with JSON mode and structured outputs
- JazzGptMidi source code: `/plugins/JazzGptMidi/Source/PluginProcessor.h` — SimplePianoVoice reference implementation
- Drop 2 voicing theory: pianowithjonny.com, learnjazzstandards.com — algorithmic description of drop-2 transformation

---

## Notes

- ChordGPT is architecturally closer to JazzGptMidi than to any traditional DSP plugin — the "DSP" is OpenAI
- No APVTS parameters simplifies implementation significantly (no WebSliderRelay, no parameter attachments)
- The WebView bridge handles all UI interaction via `withNativeFunction()` and `evaluateJavascript()`
- "New Scene" is a UI action (native function), not a parameter — clears conversation array + calls allNotesOff()
- API key storage: Use `juce::ApplicationProperties` / `juce::PropertiesFile` for maximum security (separate from DAW project state)
- ChordGPT produces both MIDI output (for DAW routing) and audio output (piano synth preview) simultaneously
- Voicing is applied post-API: system prompt informs GPT of desired voicing, JUCE engine normalizes register
- Token management: Full conversation history kept; practical limit ~30 exchanges before context grows unwieldy (manageable with chord-only responses)
